{
  "criterion": "architecture",

  "rationale": "Backend has clean layering with the rubric's expected directories: policy/ (pure rules + constants), services/ (db + business logic), routes/ (HTTP), db/ (connection/seed), and a small named error hierarchy in errors.ts (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, PaymentRequiredError, NotFoundError). All business constants live in policy/constants.ts (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS=5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS=14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY=50, etc.) and are imported via policy/index.ts — no duplication seen. The HTTP layer (loan-handler.ts) defers to loanService and translates domain errors to HTTP codes; it contains zero SQL. madge.json reports no circular deps and no orphans. File-size distribution is healthy: largest source file is MemberDetail.tsx at 219 lines, no source file exceeds 300 lines (only test files do: api.test.ts 355, policy.test.ts 230). However, this stops short of anchor 4: (a) services call db.prepare(...) directly throughout loan-service.ts (e.g., 8+ inline raw SQL statements with row casts like `as { id: string; status: string }`), with no typed query-module abstraction — services are not unit-testable in isolation; (b) frontend is organized by technical role (pages/, components/, api/, hooks/), not by feature — Checkout, BookList, MemberDetail all live flat in pages/ even though an empty features/ directory exists; (c) there is no shared types boundary — backend defines Member/Loan/Reservation interfaces in policy/index.ts while the frontend's api/index.ts (119 lines) presumably redeclares its own. Solidly anchor 3.",

parsed.rationale: Backend has clean layering with the rubric's expected directories: policy/ (pure rules + constants), services/ (db + business logic), routes/ (HTTP), db/ (connection/seed), and a small named error hierarchy in errors.ts (ConflictError, ForbiddenError, PaymentRequiredError, NotFoundError). All business constants live in policy/constants.ts (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS=5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS=14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY=50, etc.) and are imported via policy/index.ts — no duplication seen. The HTTP layer (loan-handler.ts) defers to loanService and translates domain errors to HTTP codes; it contains zero SQL. madge.json reports no circular deps and no orphans. File-size distribution is healthy: largest source file is MemberDetail.tsx at 219 lines, no source file exceeds 300 lines (only test files do: api.test.ts 355, policy.test.ts 230). However, this stops short of anchor 4: (a) services call db.prepare(...) directly throughout loan-service.ts (e.g., 8+ inline raw SQL statements with row casts like `as { id: string; status: string }`), with no typed query-module abstraction — services are not unit-testable in isolation; (b) frontend is organized by technical role (pages/, components/, api/, hooks/), not by feature — Checkout, BookList, MemberDetail all live flat in pages/ even though an empty features/ directory exists; (c) there is no shared types boundary — backend defines Member/Loan/Reservation interfaces in policy/index.ts while the frontend's api/index.ts (119 lines) presumably redeclares its own. Solidly anchor 3.
